title: China-to-Philippines BESS Compliance Gap Matrix
provenance: AI-compiled, multi-model cross-checked, not human-verified. Informational only; confirm with the official regulator and a qualified professional. Not legal/certification/customs/market-access advice.

rows.target_requirement: Philippines BESS projects should be designed and tested for the local 60 Hz grid and the applicable interconnection requirements. PCS, transformers, protection relays, auxiliary loads, HVAC, fire equipment, metering, and controls should be checked for 60 Hz operation, frequency ride-through settings, and grid-code compatibility before shipment or commissioning.
target_standards: Philippine Grid Code and applicable connection requirements
Project-specific utility or system-operator interconnection settings
60 Hz equipment ratings and test evidence
rows.china_equivalent: China domestic grid equipment is commonly designed around 50 Hz assumptions. GB/T 36558-2023 and related China grid-storage documents may support system design, but PCS and balance-of-system evidence should be rechecked for Philippines 60 Hz operation and local protection settings.
rows.gap: Identify every 50 Hz-only component and setting in the China design file. Provide 60 Hz nameplate evidence, PCS type-test settings, relay setting studies, transformer and auxiliary-load ratings, and updated FAT / SAT procedures for the Philippines grid.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory as part of grid connection, energisation, and equipment safety where local 60 Hz interconnection requirements apply
mandatory_status.cn: China domestic evidence may be 50 Hz-based unless the export configuration is specifically tested and rated for 60 Hz

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Philippines export configurations should be proven at 60 Hz. A China 50 Hz grid-storage file is not enough unless the actual PCS and balance-of-system configuration is rated, tested, and set for the Philippines grid.

rows.requirement: China GB 44240-2024 / GB/T 36276-2023 Baseline vs Philippines Acceptance
rows.target_requirement: Philippines reviewers may use China GB reports as supporting technical evidence only if the importer, BPS route, ERC / grid reviewer, fire authority, project owner, insurer, or contract accepts them. China domestic compliance should not be treated as automatic Philippines compliance for standards, grid connection, ancillary services, fire safety, transport, or customs.
target_standards: Philippines BPS / PNS, ERC, grid, fire, transport, customs, and project requirements applicable to the shipment or site
IEC or PNS documents where required by the Philippines approval route
rows.china_equivalent: The GB-standard cn_common_equivalent baseline for China BESS commonly includes GB 44240-2024 for lithium-ion cells and batteries for electric energy storage, GB/T 36276-2023 for electric-energy-storage lithium-ion batteries, and GB/T 36558-2023 for electrochemical energy storage system technical requirements.
rows.gap: The gap is recognition and scope. Create a traceable matrix showing which China GB clauses cover the requested Philippines, PNS, IEC, grid, fire, transport, or project clauses, and clearly mark any evidence that is only China domestic, IEC-aligned, project-owner accepted, or locally approved.

mandatory_status.target: Philippines acceptance is mandatory only where a Philippines authority, importer, grid operator, fire authority, customs route, insurer, or contract requires the mapped evidence; China GB reports alone are not confirmed as a mandatory or sufficient Philippines approval route
mandatory_status.cn: GB 44240-2024 is mandatory for covered China domestic electric-energy-storage lithium-ion cells and batteries; GB/T 36276-2023 and GB/T 36558-2023 are commonly used recommended technical references

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Use GB 44240-2024, GB/T 36276-2023, and GB/T 36558-2023 as China baseline evidence, not as automatic Philippines compliance. Acceptance must be confirmed by the specific BPS, ERC, grid, fire, transport, customs, or contract path.

source.publisher: State Administration for Market Regulation Standard Public Service Platform

rows.requirement: Philippines Fire Code Review for BESS Sites
rows.target_requirement: BESS installations in the Philippines should be reviewed under the Revised Fire Code of the Philippines, local building and electrical permitting, and any site-specific Bureau of Fire Protection or local authority requirements. The fire package should address battery-room or container layout, separation, ventilation, detection, suppression, emergency shutdown, access, signage, and emergency response. Do not overclaim a single national BESS product fire certificate unless a project instrument specifically requires it.
target_standards: Republic Act No. 9514, Revised Fire Code of the Philippines of 2008
Implementing rules and local fire-safety permit requirements
Project-specific BFP or local authority review
rows.china_equivalent: China commonly relies on GB 44240-2024, GB/T 36276-2023, GB/T 36558-2023, and local project fire review for stationary energy storage. These documents may support hazard analysis but do not replace Philippines fire-code, building, electrical, or local authority review.
rows.gap: Prepare a Philippines site fire-safety file, not only a product datasheet. Include thermal runaway mitigation, battery management protections, fire detection and suppression design, ventilation or gas management, container spacing, emergency isolation, firefighting access, and local-language emergency procedures.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory where fire-code, building, electrical, BFP, local authority, insurer, or project-owner review applies to the installation; product-level IEC or GB testing alone is not sufficient
mandatory_status.cn: Mandatory or project-specific depending on Chinese project approval and local fire review

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Philippines BESS fire compliance is site and approval-route dependent. A China GB or IEC battery report should be used as supporting evidence, not as a replacement for fire-code review.

source.publisher: Official Gazette of the Republic of the Philippines

rows.requirement: ERC Ancillary Services and Grid-Storage Approval Path
rows.target_requirement: Philippines grid-scale BESS may require project-specific ERC, DOE, system-operator, market, and grid-connection review before energisation or participation in ancillary services. BESS used for reserve, frequency response, or other ancillary services should be checked against the applicable ERC-approved procurement, market, interconnection, metering, dispatch, and qualification documents for that project.
target_standards: ERC-approved ancillary-services and reserve-market instruments where applicable
Philippine Grid Code and connection requirements applicable to the project
Project-specific system-operator and market registration requirements
rows.china_equivalent: China grid-storage evidence commonly includes GB/T 36558-2023 for electrochemical energy storage system technical requirements, GB/T 36276-2023 for storage lithium-ion batteries, and grid-company commissioning documents. These documents support engineering review but do not replace Philippines ERC, system-operator, market, or interconnection approval.
rows.gap: Separate product compliance from project qualification. Prepare PCS performance curves, battery limits, control response data, metering design, communication interfaces, protection studies, dispatch logic, and commissioning evidence for the Philippines grid and ancillary-services route.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory where the BESS connects to the grid, participates in WESM or ancillary services, or is covered by an ERC, DOE, system-operator, utility, market, or contract approval route; not a blanket product certificate
mandatory_status.cn: Mandatory or project-specific depending on Chinese grid-company connection, dispatch, and commissioning requirements

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] A China GB battery file does not qualify a BESS for Philippines grid connection or ancillary services. ERC, grid, market, and project documents must be checked for the specific site and service.

rows.requirement: BPS / PNS Adoption or Acceptance of IEC 62619:2022 / IEC 62933 Evidence
rows.target_requirement: DTI-BPS is the Philippines National Standards Body and provides the official route to search or purchase Philippine National Standards. For BESS, exporters should confirm whether the project, importer, tender, insurer, or authority requires a PNS adoption of IEC 62619:2022 for industrial secondary lithium cells and batteries, an IEC 62933 electrical energy storage system standard, or direct IEC evidence. The public BPS mandatory-certification list should also be checked because it does not by itself establish a blanket whole-unit lithium BESS mandatory certification route.
target_standards: PNS adoption or acceptance of IEC 62619:2022 where confirmed
PNS adoption or acceptance of IEC 62933 series where confirmed
BPS List of Products Under Mandatory Certification
rows.china_equivalent: China commonly uses GB 44240-2024 and GB/T 36276-2023 for lithium-ion cells and batteries for electric energy storage. These GB-standard documents may support a Philippines technical file but should be mapped to the exact PNS or IEC standard requested rather than treated as automatic BPS acceptance.
rows.gap: Do not ship on the assumption that a China GB report is a Philippines PNS certificate. Build a clause matrix from GB 44240-2024 and GB/T 36276-2023 to the requested IEC or PNS documents, identify the exact edition, laboratory accreditation, cell and module configuration, and any missing system-level evidence.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory only where a BPS technical regulation, PNS adoption, importer route, tender, insurer, authority, or contract calls up the exact evidence; otherwise IEC 62619:2022 / IEC 62933 evidence is a technical due-diligence or project requirement
mandatory_status.cn: GB 44240-2024 is mandatory where applicable in China; GB/T 36276-2023 is recommended and commonly used for BESS battery evidence

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Treat IEC 62619:2022 and IEC 62933 as Philippines project or PNS evidence only after confirming the exact adoption, edition, and approval route with BPS, the importer, or the project reviewer.

source.publisher: Department of Trade and Industry - Bureau of Philippine Standards (DTI-BPS)

rows.target_requirement: Lithium cells and batteries shipped to the Philippines normally need dangerous-goods transport documentation aligned with UN Manual of Tests and Criteria subsection 38.3, including the test summary and classification details required by the chosen air, sea, or land transport route. This is a transport safety file, not a substitute for BPS, ERC, grid, or fire approval.
target_standards: UN Manual of Tests and Criteria, subsection 38.3
Applicable modal dangerous-goods rules for air, sea, or land transport
rows.china_equivalent: China export files often include UN 38.3 reports, lithium battery test summaries, MSDS / SDS, and transport classification documents. GB 44240-2024 and GB/T 36276-2023 battery safety reports are useful product evidence but do not replace UN 38.3 transport evidence.
rows.gap: Confirm that the UN 38.3 test summary covers the same cell, battery, module, or pack configuration being shipped, and that it matches the shipping name, UN number, state of charge limits, packaging instructions, and carrier requirements for the route to the Philippines.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory for regulated lithium battery transport where dangerous-goods rules apply; not a whole-system market-access approval
mandatory_status.cn: Mandatory for regulated lithium battery transport from China where dangerous-goods rules apply

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] UN 38.3 is a shipment gate for lithium batteries, but it does not prove Philippines BPS, ERC, grid, or fire compliance for a BESS project.

source.publisher: United Nations Economic Commission for Europe (UNECE)
